name: int4-mtp-bi0-fp8kv
description: NEGATIVE RESULT (PR #777): fp8 KV cache is not serviceable on A10G/sm_86 for the int4-compressed-tensors + force-2D-TRITON_ATTN + torch.compile stack. Built as int4_mtp_bi0_surgattn + --kv-cache-dtype, but no fp8 dtype boots: fp8_e5m2 is rejected by vLLM's _init_kv_cache_quant guard (relaxed here by vllm_fp8kv_e5m2_guard_patch.py) then by the attention-forward assert kv_cache_dtype in {fp8,fp8_e4m3,nvfp4}; fp8_e4m3/fp8 pass vLLM but fail Triton compile (fp8e4nv unsupported on sm_86, supported set is fp8e4b15/fp8e5); int8 is not a valid vLLM CacheDType. The two Ampere-compilable Triton fp8 dtypes are exactly the two the attention kernel refuses. KV_CACHE_DTYPE defaults to 'auto' => default bf16 KV == the exact bi0 surgattn 218.02-TPS baseline (the only runnable config); fp8_e5m2/fp8_e4m3 remain selectable only to reproduce the three-layer wall. Re-confirms the prior fp8-KV dead-end (wirbel #141; BASELINE.md). Everything else byte-for-byte bi0 surgattn: VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=0, force-2D TRITON_ATTN patch, gemma4_assistant MTP drafter NUM_SPECULATIVE_TOKENS=6, int4 W4A16 weights, all modalities.
